mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate compresses equity discount rates and lowers corporate borrowing costs, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9.5 percent indicates severe labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in greater than 55 percent of triggered months, or rolling IC exceeds 0.05 despite persistently high unemployment readings.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand recovery, which drives positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns with accuracy above 52 percent, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid industrial production growth clears supply chain bottlenecks and accelerates short-term corporate revenue realization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 65 percent of months when INDPRO month-over-month growth exceeds 1.0 percent, or short-horizon IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of policy rates below 0.5 percent and unemployment above 9.0 percent creates a liquidity trap that decouples equity valuations from earnings, producing negative forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day returns are positive in greater than 60 percent of triggered periods, or the interaction effect reverses sign in walk-forward validation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth momentum directly translates to corporate revenue expansion and margin improvement, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth fails to predict 20-day return direction with accuracy above 52 percent, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.03 over consecutive 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll increase exceeding 150,000 signals labor market tightening that boosts consumer spending confidence and corporate hiring, triggering positive short-term equity momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 58 percent of months following strong payroll prints, or short-horizon predictive power vanishes after controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Falling consumer price index readings reduce real interest rates and ease financial conditions, which compresses equity discount rates and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Declining CPI fails to predict positive 20-day returns with accuracy above 50 percent, or the signal reverses direction during stagflationary periods without regime filtering.
